title: Mailchimp vs MailerLite (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp and MailerLite: pricing, features, ease of use, and which email marketing tool suits your business best.
h1: Mailchimp vs MailerLite: Detailed Feature & Price Comparison
intro: Both Mailchimp and MailerLite are popular freemium email marketing platforms, but they cater to slightly different audiences. This comparison helps small business owners and freelancers decide which platform offers better value, features, and ease of use for their email campaigns.
choose_a_if: You need an established platform with extensive third-party integrations and advanced marketing automation
You're looking for landing page functionality as part of a broader marketing suite
You want affiliate program opportunities with recurring 30% commissions
choose_b_if: You're budget-conscious and need more free subscribers (1,000 vs 500)
You run an e-commerce business and want built-in shopping integrations
You prefer a simpler, more intuitive interface designed specifically for small businesses
You want better customer support and reliability (evidenced by higher Trustpilot rating of 4.5 vs 1.5)

faq.a: It depends on your needs. MailerLite offers better value for budget-conscious users and e-commerce businesses, with a higher free tier (1,000 vs 500 subscribers) and lower paid pricing ($9 vs $13). Mailchimp excels for those needing extensive third-party integrations and advanced marketing features. User reviews favor MailerLite significantly (4.5 Trustpilot vs 1.5).

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or MailerLite?
faq.a: MailerLite is the more affordable option, starting at $9/month compared to Mailchimp's $13/month. Additionally, MailerLite's free tier supports 1,000 subscribers versus Mailchimp's 500, making it better for growing businesses that want to delay paid plans longer.

faq.q: Can I switch from MailerLite to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow you to export your subscriber lists and campaigns. Most email marketing tools support CSV imports, making migration straightforward. However, you may lose some automation workflows and custom formatting during the transfer, so plan accordingly.
